name: Regional Loan Corporation
description_short: Non-profit SBA 504 lender in Louisiana offering fixed-rate small business financing with low equity requirements and 25-year terms.
description_long:
Regional Loan Corporation was established in 1978 as a private non-profit organization dedicated to stimulating economic development and job creation across Louisiana. The organization has evolved into a certified Small Business Development Company that collaborates with area banks, government agencies, and economic development organizations to provide alternative financing sources for entrepreneurs. Over its 45+ year history, RLC has deployed more than $100 million in loans to over 350 businesses across diverse industries including manufacturing, wholesale, hospitality, food service, retail, and auto repair.

Regional Loan Corporation specializes in SBA 504 loans and manages multiple government-sponsored financing programs including the Restore Louisiana Program, EDA/LED revolving loan funds, and Business Resource Capital (BRC) offerings. Their primary product is the SBA 504 program, currently advertised at 6.45-6.82% fixed interest rates with 25-year terms. The company targets small, female, and minority-owned businesses across 13 Louisiana parishes including Orleans, Jefferson, St. Tammany, Tangipahoa, and Terrebonne counties. Applicants can access fillable loan applications online and work with the organization's team in New Orleans.

What distinguishes RLC is its dual focus on long-term fixed-rate financing with unusually low borrower equity requirements—a significant advantage compared to traditional commercial bank lending. By functioning as a non-profit SBDC, the organization prioritizes community economic development over profit maximization. Their portfolio breadth across manufacturing, hospitality, and retail demonstrates real-world lending experience across varied business types. The 25-year term option provides exceptional repayment flexibility for asset-based businesses.

Potential borrowers should note that RLC's service area is geographically limited to specific Louisiana parishes and that SBA 504 loans require substantial collateral and typically longer application timelines than conventional bank loans. While interest rates are competitive, they remain subject to monthly changes based on SBA debenture sales. The organization's non-profit status and government partnership structure may result in more conservative underwriting standards than alternative lenders, though specific approval rates are not disclosed.
pros: SBA 504 loans with long-term fixed rates (6.45-6.82%) locked for 25 years, providing payment stability
Low borrower equity requirements compared to conventional small business loans
Established non-profit track record: $100 million deployed to 350+ businesses since 1978
Serves specific underrepresented groups including female and minority-owned businesses
Covers diverse industries (manufacturing, hospitality, retail, food service, auto repair)
Collaborates with area banks, reducing borrower friction and improving access
Government-backed programs (SBA, EDA, LED) provide regulatory stability and legitimacy
cons: Limited geographic service area: only 13 specific Louisiana parishes, excluding most of the United States
Interest rates subject to monthly changes based on SBA debenture sales, not fully predictable at application
SBA 504 loans typically require significant collateral and longer application/approval timelines than alternative lenders
No specific information provided about approval rates, credit score requirements, or time-to-funding
Primarily asset-based lending program; may not accommodate businesses without real estate or equipment collateral
best_for: Small manufacturing or food service businesses in Louisiana seeking 15-25 year asset financing
Female and minority-owned business owners in target parishes needing below-market fixed rates
Established businesses with real estate or equipment collateral unable to qualify for conventional bank loans
Economic development initiatives in rural Louisiana parishes (Assumption, Lafourche, Terrebonne, Washington)
diagnosis: Regional Loan Corporation is ideal for established Louisiana small businesses with real estate or equipment collateral seeking long-term (20-25 year) fixed-rate financing at competitive rates. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to 13 specific Louisiana parishes and the requirement for substantial tangible assets to serve as collateral; SBA 504 loans also involve longer underwriting timelines than alternative products.
services: Collaboration with area banks for co-lending
Economic development financing across 13 Louisiana parishes
